pages.title: Who’s That Snake (Funky Slow-Jam Mix)
pages.tagline: Dusty boom-bap truth against identity thieves.

pages.body: Step into a dusty lounge where thick electric bass grooves and loose swing set the pace for a witty deconstruction of family trees gone wrong. This track navigates complex circles of clones and corporate wolves, using clean male call-and-response vocals to separate truth from perception. From Rhodes chords sliding through warm melodies to occasional horn stabs cutting through subtle vinyl crackle, every scratch in the hook adds texture to this laid-back East Coast narrative. It is a journey through crumbling history where media manipulation meets generational trauma, inviting you to sort out too many names found in files while keeping that funky slow-jam energy alive.
pages.styleSentence: A laid-back early-90s East Coast boom-bap track at roughly 88 BPM featuring thick funky electric bass grooves with melodic slides, loose dusty drum swing, warm Rhodes chords, occasional horn stabs, subtle vinyl crackle, and classic DJ scratches in the hook.

pages.text:
[Intro - Codec Static]
Uh, check it.
Too many snakes in the grass?
Nah.
Too many names in the file.
Let’s sort it out.

[Hook]
They want the facts, so we give ’em the facts
Who’s that Snake? Let me bring it right back
One was the student, one was the boss
One was the clone who refused that loss
One had the anger, one had the blades
One wore the mask in another man’s name
Round and round, but the tree don’t lie
Now everybody say, “Codec live!”

[Verse 1]
Diggity-deep in the jungle, the story gets humble
A soldier met a teacher, then the whole world crumbled
She was the root, the truth, the pain in the boot
The one who taught the moves, then got framed by the suit

He was the student with the sneaky little mission
Crawling through the grass with a Cold War condition
Naked was the name before the legend got tossed
Then the medal hit heavy, now they call him Big Boss

Not the same as the clone with the bandana tied
Not the same as the blond with the venom inside
Not the same as the president swinging both blades
This is daddy of the drama from the old-school days

[Hook]
They want the facts, so we give ’em the facts
Who’s that Snake? Let me bring it right back
One was the student, one was the boss
One was the clone who refused that loss
One had the anger, one had the blades
One wore the mask in another man’s name
Round and round, but the tree don’t lie
Now everybody say, “Codec live!”

[Verse 2]
Now listen to the clone zone, grown from the genome
Three sons from the legend, but none had a clean home
First one solid, quiet with the sneaky stride
Sent to stop the walking tank with the nukes inside

He ain’t the father, he ain’t the teacher
He’s the cigarette shadow with the solo feature
Snow base, gray fox, rex in the fight
He’s the one who walks away when the world turns white

Second one liquid, all rage, all flame
Mad at his brother and mad at his name
Thought his genes got cheated, thought fate picked sides
So he built up a war with revenge in his eyes

Third one solidus, suit with the swords
Former big leader cutting through cords
Wanted free will, but he scarred the next kid
Tried to beat the system with the same thing it did

[Bridge - Call and Response]
Who taught the first one?
The mentor, the root
Who became Big Boss?
The student in the suit
Who broke the war gear?
The solid one did
Who screamed “brother”?
The angry clone kid
Who swung two blades?
The third son, wild
Who wore the horn?
The phantom profile

[Verse 3]
Wiggity-wait, there’s another little riddle
A man woke up broken with a horn in the middle
Missing hand, bad dreams, memory stitched tight
Everybody said “Boss,” but the mirror said “right?”

He was the phantom, the decoy, the double
Built from the blast and baptized in trouble
Diamond Dogs bark when the whole base burns
He wears the big name while the real one turns

So horn head? Phantom.
Bandana? Solid.
Blond rage? Liquid.
Two blades? Solidus.
Eyepatch legend? Big Boss.
Teacher? The Boss.
Cowboy with the gun? Don’t trust him, he’s lost.

[Hook]
They want the facts, so we give ’em the facts
Who’s that Snake? Let me bring it right back
One was the student, one was the boss
One was the clone who refused that loss
One had the anger, one had the blades
One wore the mask in another man’s name
Round and round, but the tree don’t lie
Now everybody say, “Codec live!”

[Verse 4]
Now the cowboy kept spinning with a grin and a plan
Playing every little side like a card in his hand
Son of the mentor, but he kept that low
Triple-cross rhythm in a wild west show

Then the hidden machine put the world in a cage
War became business on a digital stage
Truth got filtered, freedom got sold
Old Snake kept walking though his body got old

So the lesson ain’t just names in a folder
It’s sons, symbols, soldiers getting colder
One chose peace, one chased pride
One fought control, one lived a lie

[Final Hook]
They want the facts, so we give ’em the facts
Who’s that Snake? Let me bring it right back
Teacher, student, father, sons
Phantom, cowboy, Patriots, guns
Solid broke cycles, Liquid chased pain
Solidus cut wires in a president’s name
Round and round, but the tree don’t lie
Now everybody say, “Codec live!”

[Outro - Spoken]
Teacher made student.
Student became legend.
Legend became father.
Father became clones.
One chose freedom.
One chose rage.
One chose rebellion.
One wore the mask.

Codec closed.
Now you know.

pages.vocalStyle: Clean male duo vocals with call-and-response energy

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Narrates the crumbling history. Leads the call-and-response truth.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Supports the lead voice. Adds depth to every harmonic layer.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Keeps the floor shaking with loose swing and dusty groove.

members.name: PERCUSSION
members.bio: Adds texture without overpowering the funky bassline rhythms.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Grooves thick electric lines through melodic slides always on time.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Slides warm chords that define the laid-back slow-jam mood.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Crackles softly with static to enhance the retro tape vibe.

members.name: GUITAR
members.bio: Plays subtle scratches in the hook for classic boom-bap flavor.

members.name: KEYS
members.bio: Stabs horns occasionally to punctuate the narrative storytelling.

levels.name: TWISTED FACTS COOLDOWN
levels.emotion: Resigned acceptance with a final twist

levels.name: CRUMBLING HISTORY
levels.emotion: Narrative storytelling with a sense of crumbling history

levels.name: CLONE EXPOSITION ZONE
levels.emotion: Angry exposition on genetic and ideological clones

levels.name: FRAGMENTED MEMORY
levels.emotion: Chaos of fragmented memories and identity loss
